programmes.show_description: Fourth and final part of the definitive critical review of the music of one of history's most influential rock bands, Led Zeppelin, on stage, on record and on film. (4 of 4)

programmes.show_name: The Doors: Live At The Bowl `68
programmes.show_description: At the height of their musical prowess, The Doors perform all their classic hits during their legendary 1968 concert at the Hollywood Bowl.
A fascinating look at American rock band The Doors. Led by Jim Morrison, they were one of the most revolutionary and influential bands of the 60s. (S5, ep 18)
Artworks by Gustav Klimt, Amedeo Modigliani, Pablo Picasso and Camille Pissarro are expected to fetch tens of millions of pounds at Sotheby's. (S8, ep 9)
Artworks by Boudin, Matisse, Dali and and Monet go on offer at Christie's, as does Paul Gauguin's Te Fare, which is expected to fetch up to £18million. (S8, ep 10)
A stunning performance of Madama Butterfly from Bregenz, as Giacomo Puccini's powerful opera presents an enduring, tragic tale of unrequited love.
The Colonel's Lady: Colonel George Peregrine's comfortable life is disturbed when his wife writes a bestselling raunchy novel. Is this evidence that she's having an affair? (S9, ep 2)
The Surgeon: When wealthy Prince Zawi gives surgeon Robert Sandy a diamond for saving his life, the doctor finds that his new jewel is really more of a liability than an asset. (S9, ep 3)
The Verger: When devoted verger Albert is forced to resign, he sets out to build a new life. But can he succeed in a modern world that has little time for his old-fashioned ways? (S9, ep 4)
The Facts Of Life: When sheltered young man Nicholas travels to London to compete in a fencing competition, he gets caught up in a very strange adventure. (S9, ep 5)
Wink Three Times: When a timid provincial solicitor visits a smart London hotel on a business trip, he becomes embroiled in a curious case of mistaken identity. (S9, ep 6)
The Jar: Charlie buys a strange jar at a carnival. His wife is scared of it, but townspeople come from miles to see it and Charlie becomes a local celebrity. (S2, ep 17)
Final Escape: A convict plans a clever escape with the help of an alcoholic fellow prisoner who's in charge of making coffins and burying the camp's deceased. (S2, ep 18)
With contributions from members of the band, hear the definitive story of the Beach Boys - one of the most successful bands ever who went toe to toe with the Beatles.
After a man loses his job due to his employer's whimsical daughter, a young boy helps him find work as a singer at a local hotel.
The Falkirk Wheel: Stephen Mangan hosts the grand finale with just three artists who have made it through to the last round of the competition. (S11, ep 8)
Winner's Film: Join the winner of this year's Sky Arts Landscape Artist of the Year as they travel to County Mayo to research their £10,000 prize commission. (S11, ep 9)
Solsbury Hill and Sledgehammer singer Peter Gabriel brings his 1987 tour to a climactic close in an electrifying concert at the hillside open air theatre at Lycabettus in Athens.

programmes.show_name: Phil Collins: Going Back To Detroit
programmes.show_description: Genesis hit machine Phil Collins teams up with three original Detroit session guitarists to recreate the original Motown Sound for his 2010 album Going Back.

programmes.show_name: Electric Light Orchestra: Live - The Early Years
programmes.show_description: A compilation of three ELO concerts: Fusion Live in London (1976), plus unreleased shows at Brunel University (1973) and Germany's Rockpalast (1974).
Pink Floyd take to the stage with their final live show of their 1994 Division Bell Tour for a performance of some of their greatest hits and new songs. Flashing images.
...Simon, David Gilmour, Sheryl Crow. A blend of global icons from a critically acclaimed series, featuring performances in the historic Abbey Road Studios.
...Hot Chili Peppers, Green Day, Muse. A blend of global icons perform live from the historic Abbey Road Studios, showcasing some of the most exciting sets from over the years.
Anthem To Beauty: Through intimate interviews, the Grateful Dead reveal how they created seminal albums American Beauty and Anthem of the Sun. (S1, ep 7)
Join Ratty, Badger, Mole and the impulsive Toad as they embark on a series of riotous adventures spiralling from Toad's insatiable need for speed.
The Finger Of Suspicion: Happily married and living in the Middle East, American safe-cracker Steve is suddenly threatened with extradition for a crime he says he didn't commit. (S9, ep 7)
A Time To Die: Yves's marriage has gone stale and his young mistress has announced that she is pregnant. But he then makes a decision which changes everyone's lives forever. (S9, ep 8)
Mr Know-All: When pretty hotel worker Elly is charmed by a guest, archaeologist Max Kelada, it seems that the Mr Know-All professor might not be the man he claims to be. (S9, ep 9)
The Man From The South: A young American couple on holiday meet a mysterious old gentleman who makes them a macabre wager. (S1, ep 1)
Mrs Bixby and the Colonel's Coat: Unable to show her dentist husband Cyril the fur coat her lover has given her, Mrs Bixby proceeds to hatch a clever plan. (S1, ep 2)
Murder Case: An actor learns that his ex, an actress, is now married to her play's backer. They rekindle their romance and plot to get her out of her marriage. (S2, ep 19)
The Falkirk Wheel: Stephen Mangan hosts the grand finale with just three artists who have made it through to the last round of the competition. (S11, ep 8)
Winner's Film: Join the winner of this year's Sky Arts Landscape Artist of the Year as they travel to County Mayo to research their £10,000 prize commission. (S11, ep 9)

programmes.show_name: Celine Dion: Taking Chances World Tour
programmes.show_description: The iconic singer performs an electrifying set filled with hits, including tracks from her album Taking Chances and a stunning rendition of My Heart Will Go On.
Hank Marvin, Bruce Welch and Brian Bennett are reunited for The Shadows' final tour in 2004, treating crowds to a medley of their greatest hits, including Apache, Living Doll and FBI.
Documentary film by Margy Kinmonth shining a spotlight on trailblazing female war artists on the front lines, with interviews of artists talking about and showcasing their work.
An American POW in the Korean War is brainwashed as an unwitting assassin for an international Communist conspiracy. Discriminatory content. (1962)(130 mins)
Prepare to be blown away by performances from Opry member Trisha Yearwood, Jordan Davis and Marcus King.
